title: Red Curry Chicken with Rice Noodles
intro: This dish draws from Thai red curry technique and Chinese velveting methods. Curry paste is fried hard for depth, chicken is velveted tenderness, and the sauce is reduced to cling to noodles rather than pool beneath them.

ingredientGroups.title: Velveted chicken
items: 2 to 3 boneless skinless chicken thighs (about 12 oz)
½ tsp kosher salt
½ tsp freshly cracked black pepper
¼ tsp cayenne
¼ tsp smoked paprika
1½ tsp cornstarch
1 tsp low-sodium soy sauce

ingredientGroups.title: Vegetables and aromatics
items: 1 large yellow onion
1 large bok choy
1 cup baby corn
3 cloves garlic
3 inches fresh ginger
1 lime

ingredientGroups.title: Curry sauce
items: 3 Tbsp red curry paste
2 Tbsp light coconut milk
1½ cups chicken stock
1½ Tbsp low-sodium soy sauce
1 tsp rice vinegar

ingredientGroups.title: Noodles
items: 8 oz flat rice noodles
1 tsp kosher salt (for noodle water)
½ tsp sesame oil

ingredientGroups.title: Cooking oils
items: 1½ Tbsp olive oil
1 Tbsp olive oil

ingredientGroups.title: Finishing
items: ½ cup Thai basil or ⅓ cup sweet Italian basil plus 1 Tbsp fresh mint
1 tsp chili crisp (crunchy chili oil with garlic and spices)

stepGroups.title: Velvet the Chicken
items: Thinly slice 12 oz boneless skinless chicken thighs across the grain into bite-size strips.
Place the chicken in a medium bowl and add ½ tsp kosher salt, ½ tsp freshly cracked black pepper, ¼ tsp cayenne, ¼ tsp smoked paprika, 1½ tsp cornstarch, and 1 tsp low-sodium soy sauce.
Toss thoroughly until each piece is evenly coated and no dry starch remains visible.
Let rest at room temperature for 15 minutes.
This resting period allows the cornstarch to hydrate and adhere to the meat, ensuring a silky texture and tender finish when cooked.

stepGroups.title: Prepare the Mise en Place
items: Thinly slice 1 large yellow onion.
Trim 1 large bok choy. Separate the stalks from the leafy tops. Slice the stalks thinly and discard the leaves.
Cut 1 can baby corn into ¾ inch pieces.
Mince 3 cloves garlic.
Peel and slice 3 inches of fresh ginger into paper-thin coins.
Zest 1 lime.
Tear ½ cup Thai basil and set aside for finishing.
Measure 3 Tbsp red curry paste.
Measure 2 Tbsp light coconut milk and 1½ cups chicken stock.
Measure 1½ Tbsp low-sodium soy sauce and 1 tsp rice vinegar.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil and add 1 tsp kosher salt.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Chicken
items: Place the wok over high heat and allow it to become very hot.
Add 1½ Tbsp olive oil and swirl to coat the bottom and lower sides.
Immediately add the velveted chicken in a single layer, spreading it across the hottest surface. Do not overcrowd; cook in batches if necessary.
Let the chicken sear undisturbed for about 45 seconds to 1 minute to develop light browning.
Begin stir-frying, tossing and spreading the pieces so they cook evenly.
Cook just until the chicken is opaque and lightly caramelized at the edges, about 2 to 3 minutes total.
Transfer to a plate and set aside.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Noodles
items: Add 8 oz rice noodles to the boiling salted water.
Cook 1½ minutes less than the package directions.
Drain immediately and toss with ½ tsp sesame oil to prevent sticking. Set aside.

stepGroups.title: Char the Vegetables
items: Return the wok to high heat and allow it to become very hot.
Add 1 Tbsp olive oil and swirl to coat the bottom and lower sides.
Add the sliced onion and bok choy stalks and the baby corn.
Spread them across the hottest surface and let sit undisturbed for 30 to 45 seconds to begin charring.
Stir-fry for 2 to 3 minutes, tossing and spreading the vegetables so some edges blister while others soften.
Add the minced garlic and sliced ginger.
Stir-fry for about 30 seconds, just until fragrant. Do not allow the garlic or ginger to brown deeply.

stepGroups.title: Fry the Curry Paste
items: Keep the wok over high heat.
Push the vegetables up the sides of the wok, clearing the center to expose the hottest surface.
Add 3 Tbsp red curry paste directly to the center.
If the wok looks dry, drizzle in 1 to 2 teaspoons of oil from the sides to help it fry.
Let the paste sit undisturbed for 20 to 30 seconds.
Using a spatula, press and spread the paste against the hot metal.
Stir and continue frying for about 2 minutes total, allowing it to darken slightly and become deeply fragrant.
Once fully fried, toss the vegetables back through the paste to coat.

stepGroups.title: Build and Reduce the Sauce
items: With the vegetables coated in the fried curry paste, pour in 1½ cups chicken stock and 2 Tbsp light coconut milk.
Stir immediately, scraping the bottom of the wok to dissolve any browned bits.
Bring the liquid to a steady boil, with consistent bubbling across the surface.
Cook for 2 to 3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce reduces slightly and looks glossy.
Add 1½ Tbsp low-sodium soy sauce, 1 tsp rice vinegar, and the zest of 1 lime.
Stir to combine.

stepGroups.title: Combine the Chicken and Noodles
items: Return the cooked chicken to the wok and toss briefly to warm through.
Add the drained noodles.
Using tongs or a wok spatula, lift and toss continuously to coat the noodles in the sauce and distribute the vegetables evenly.
Continue cooking over high heat for 1 to 2 minutes, allowing the noodles to absorb some of the liquid.
If the mixture looks dry before the noodles are evenly coated, add a small splash of chicken stock and toss again.

stepGroups.title: Finish and Serve
items: Remove the wok from the heat.
Add ½ cup torn Thai basil and 1 tsp chili crisp.
Toss gently just until the basil wilts and becomes fragrant.
Squeeze the juice of the zested lime over the noodles and toss once more to brighten the sauce.
Serve immediately.
